channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Mystery solved: Anthropic reveals changes to Claude's harnesses and operating instructions likely caused degradation
item.description: For several weeks, a growing chorus of developers and AI power users claimed that Anthropic’s flagship models were losing their edge. Users across GitHub, X, and Reddit reported a phenomenon they described as "AI shrinkflation"—a perceived degradation where Claude seemed less capable of sustained reasoning, more prone to hallucinations, and increasingly wasteful with tokens. Critics pointed to a measurable shift in behavior, alleging that the model had moved from a "research-first" approach to a lazier, "edit-first" style that could no longer be trusted for complex engineering. While the compa...

item.title: Brendan Carr’s war on wokeness targets inclusive children’s television
item.description: Under the guidance of consummate bully / chairman Brendan Carr, the FCC is taking steps towards cracking down on children's entertainment that in any way explores the complexities of gender identity. On Wednesday, the FCC's Media Bureau announced that it is soliciting comments from the public about whether the TV ratings system has made sound […]

item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

item.title: Australia joins countries trialing Claude Mythos 'to ​make sure we are aware of emerging vulnerabilities'
item.description: The company is "working with" Anthropic and other similar software providers, a spokesperson said.

item.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
item.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

item.title: Exclusive: BDX Droid figures, a talking Mandalorian, and more Star Wars toys are landing at Disney Store for May the 4th
item.description: Disney Store’s May the 4th drop includes exclusive BDX Droid figures, a talking Mandalorian action figure, light-up gauntlets, collectible pins, and more inspired by The Mandalorian and Grogu.

item.title: Thermal Master P4 review: Easily the best phone-connected thermal camera yet, but it's not perfect
item.description: The Thermal Master P4 is a thermal camera that connects to an Android phone or PC using USB.

item.title: A former employee is suing MrBeast’s company for harassment, discrimination
item.description: Beast Industries denies the claims in the lawsuit, filed in North Carolina Tuesday. Beast Industries, the $5 billion media conglomerate founded by YouTube star MrBeast, is being sued by a former employee who says she was sexually harassed, discriminated against as a woman, and fired shortly after returning from maternity leave.

item.title: Rednote Draws a Line Between China and the World
item.description: As the platform expands abroad, it’s taking steps to separate Chinese users from the international audiences it once brought together.

item.title: FCC challenges gender identity themes on kids TV
item.description: The federal agency calls into question entire system of TV ratings.

item.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
item.description: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly offering voluntary buyouts to up to 7 percent of its employees
item.description: Microsoft is planning to get rid of more US employees via its first voluntary buyout program, CNBC reports. The buyout program will reportedly be offered to US employees at "the senior director level and below whose years of employment and age add up to 70 or higher," and could cover up to 7 percent of the company's US workforce. With around 125,000 employees in the US as of June 2025, that could mean up to 8,750 will be offered a paid exit when Microsoft begins its program in May. That's a smaller figure than the 15,000 or so employees the company laid off in May and July of 2025, but still s...

item.title: Spotify is celebrating its 20th anniversary, and so are we — these are 5 of the platform’s landmark moments and yes, one of them involves Taylor Swift
item.description: These are 5 of Spotify's most memorable moments from the last 20 years.

item.title: The dark side of AI —shocking report reveals 11 planned AI data centers connected to OpenAI, Meta and Microsoft could produce more greenhouse gases than Morocco
item.description: New AI data centers could emit more greenhouse gases than an entire nation.

item.title: Meta will lay off 10% of workforce, company told staff today
item.description: As Meta spends heavy on AI initiatives and pushes for efficiency, 8,000 employees will be cut and 6,000 open roles shut. Meta announced to employees on Thursday that the company is laying off around 10% of the company on May 20—about 8,000 employees out of its workforce of more than 78,000 will be impacted, Bloomberg first reported.

item.title: No, Capital One didn’t bankroll an influencer’s wedding—but if you have enough followers, a big brand might sponsor yours
item.description: The cost of nuptials keeps skyrocketing. And while there’s little relief in sight for most of us, some brides and grooms are softening the blow with spon-con. Modern brides need something old, new, borrowed, and blue. But if they’re influencers, add a sponsor to the list, too.
